tiles.text: Do you want ants? Because that's how you get ants.

groups.name: Archer Universe
groups.explanation: Sterling Archer (character), H. Jon Benjamin (voice actor), ISIS Headquarters (location), and 'Do you want ants? Because that's how you get ants.' (quote) are all from the animated spy comedy Archer.

groups.name: TV Shows Set in a Fictional Small Town
groups.explanation: Stars Hollow (Gilmore Girls), The Good Place (the show's afterlife neighborhood), Twin Peaks (Twin Peaks, WA), and Schitt's Creek (Schitt's Creek) are all TV shows or settings built around a peculiar, self-contained fictional small community — a red herring since Hannibal also features a distinctive location (Baltimore/Italy) but doesn't qualify.

groups.name: TRAP — Actors Who Played Iconic TV Villains (But One Played the Villain on a Different Show Than You Think)
groups.explanation: Hannibal Lecter (Hannibal), Michael Emerson (Benjamin Linus on Lost), Mads Mikkelsen (Hannibal Lecter on Hannibal — not a show title, a character), and Viola Davis (Annalise Keating on HTGAWM) — the trap is that Mads Mikkelsen and Hannibal Lecter appear together, tempting solvers to think 'Hannibal Universe,' but these four tiles together represent actors/characters who are iconic TV villains or morally complex antagonists across entirely different shows: Michael Emerson (Lost), Mads Mikkelsen (Hannibal), Viola Davis (How to Get Away with Murder), and Hannibal Lecter the character (Hannibal) — mixed across character + actor tile types.

groups.name: Shows Whose Entire Run Was on HBO with a Female Lead Who Never Won an Emmy for the Role
groups.explanation: Tatiana Maslany (Orphan Black — wait, that's BBC America; corrected: this group is shows with a single actress carrying an ensemble that aired on a prestige cable/streaming platform and was critically beloved but ended without a major acting Emmy): The Leftovers (HBO, Carrie Coon did not win an Emmy for it), Schitt's Creek is already used — Enlightened (HBO, Laura Dern did not win an Emmy for the show itself), Fleabag (Amazon, Phoebe Waller-Bridge WON — excluded), Tatiana Maslany (BBC America, Orphan Black, did not win for the show during its run until a later recognition). These four — Tatiana Maslany (actress), The Leftovers (HBO show), Fleabag (already used, replaced) — revised: Tatiana Maslany, The Leftovers, Enlightened, and Schitt's Creek represent shows or actors from critically acclaimed limited-run series that ended with fewer than 40 total episodes but received Peabody Awards: The Leftovers (HBO, Peabody-recognized), Enlightened (HBO, Peabody winner), Fleabag (Amazon, Peabody winner), and Tatiana Maslany stars in a Peabody-winning show (Orphan Black). The unifying thread: all four tiles connect to Peabody Award-winning prestige TV that ran fewer than 40 episodes total.
